content:
A 6 m thick clay stratum has drainage at both its top and bottom surface due to the presence of sand strata. The time to complete $50 \%$ consolidation is 2 years. The coefficient of volume change $\left(m_v\right)$ is $1.51 \times 10.3 \mathrm{~m}^2 / \mathrm{kN}$ and unit weight of water is $9.81 \mathrm{kN} / \mathrm{m}^3$.

The coefficient of permeability (in m/year) is__________ (round off to three decimal places).
explanation:
Given:

Depth of clay layer, $H = 6 \, \text{m}$

Time to complete 50% consolidation, $t = 2 \, \text{years}$

Properties:

Coefficient of volume change, $m_v = 1.51 \times 10^{-3} \, \text{m}^2/\text{kN}$

Unit weight of water, $\gamma_w = 9.81 \, \text{kN/m}^3$

Degree of consolidation, $U = 50\% = 0.5$

Since there is two-way drainage (top and bottom), the drainage path, $d$, is:

$ d = \frac{H}{2} $

The time factor for 50% consolidation is given by:

$ \left(T_V\right)_{50} = C_V \cdot \frac{t}{d^2} = \frac{k}{m_v \gamma_w} \cdot \frac{t}{d^2} $

Substituting the known values:

$ \frac{\pi}{4} \times 0.5^2 = \frac{k}{1.51 \times 10^{-3} \times 9.81} \times \frac{2}{\left(\frac{6}{2}\right)^2} $

Solving for $k$:

$ k = 0.013 \, \text{m/year} $

Therefore, the coefficient of permeability is $0.013 \, \text{m/year}$.

content: The reason(s) of the nonuniform elastic settlement profile below a flexible footing, resting on a cohesionless soil while subjected to uniform loading, is/are:

options.content: Variation of friction angle along the width of the footing
Variation of soil stiffness along the width of the footing
Variation of friction angle along the depth of the footing
Variation of soil stiffness along the depth of the footing

explanation:
The nonuniform elastic settlement profile below a flexible footing resting on a cohesionless soil while subjected to uniform loading can be attributed to variations in the physical properties of the soil. Let’s examine each option:

Option A: Variation of friction angle along the width of the footing

The friction angle is a measure of the shear strength of the soil. A variation in friction angle along the width of the footing implies that different parts of the footing are resting on soil with different shear strengths. This can lead to differential settlement across the width of the footing because areas with lower shear strength may compress more under the same load.

Option B: Variation of soil stiffness along the width of the footing

Soil stiffness, or modulus of subgrade reaction, describes how much the soil deforms under a given load. A variation in soil stiffness along the width of the footing would mean that some areas of the soil deform more easily than others. This can indeed result in a nonuniform settlement profile, as the footing will settle more in areas where the soil is less stiff.

Option C: Variation of friction angle along the depth of the footing

While a variation of friction angle with depth will affect the overall load-carrying capacity and stability of the footing, it does not directly lead to nonuniform settlement along the surface width of the footing. Instead, it influences how deep parts of the footing might penetrate the soil uniformly.

Option D: Variation of soil stiffness along the depth of the footing

Similar to option C, this variation will affect the distribution of stress and settlement with depth but not necessarily create a differential settlement profile directly along the width of the footing. It would influence how deeply different sections of the footing might settle, but in a flexible footing, the settlement profile is more influenced by lateral variations, rather than depth.

Given these explanations, the most relevant cause for a nonuniform elastic settlement profile across the width of a flexible footing is Option B: Variation of soil stiffness along the width of the footing.

content: In a triaxial unconsolidated undrained (UU) test on a saturated clay sample, the cell pressure was 100 kPa. If the deviatoric stress at failure was 150 kPa, then the undrained shear strength of the soil is _________ kPa. (in integer)
explanation:
In a UU test for clay, where the angle of internal friction ($$\phi$$) is 0 :

Given Data :

Deviatoric stress at failure ($$\sigma_d$$) : 150 kPa

Cell pressure ($$\sigma_3$$) : 100 kPa

Calculate the Major Principal Stress :

$ \sigma_1 = \sigma_3 + \sigma_d = 100 \, \text{kPa} + 150 \, \text{kPa} = 250 \, \text{kPa} $

Using the Mohr-Coulomb Failure Criterion for saturated clay (since $$\phi$$ = 0):

$ \sigma_1 = \sigma_3 \tan^2 \left( 45 + \frac{\phi}{2} \right) + 2C \tan \left( 45 + \frac{\phi}{2} \right) $

Since $$\phi$$ = 0, $$\tan^2 \left( 45 + \frac{\phi}{2} \right) = 1$$ and $$\tan \left( 45 + \frac{\phi}{2} \right) = 1$$.

Therefore :

$ \sigma_1 = \sigma_3 + 2C $

Substitute the Known Values :

$ 250 \, \text{kPa} = 100 \, \text{kPa} + 2C $

Solve for C :

$ 250 \, \text{kPa} - 100 \, \text{kPa} = 2C \\ 150 \, \text{kPa} = 2C \\ C = 75 \, \text{kPa} $

Shear Strength Calculation :

The undrained shear strength ($$\tau$$) is :

$ \tau = C + \overline{\sigma_n} \tan \phi = 75 \, \text{kPa} \quad (since \, \phi = 0) $

Therefore, the undrained shear strength of the soil is 75 kPa.

content: A consolidated drained (CD) triaxial test was carried out on a sand sample with the known effective shear strength parameters, 𝑐′ = 0 and 𝜙′ = 30°. In the test, prior to the failure, when the sample was undergoing axial compression under constant cell pressure, the drainage valve was accidentally closed. At the failure, 360 kPa deviatoric stress was recorded along with 70 kPa pore water pressure. If the test is repeated without such error, and no back pressure is applied in either of the tests, what is the deviatoric stress (in kPa, in integer) at the failure? ___________
explanation:
Consolidated Drained (CD) Test:

  • In a Consolidated Drained (CD) test on a saturated soil sample, drainage is allowed throughout the test. This means both the normal stress and shear stress are applied gradually to ensure full consolidation, preventing any excess pore pressure from developing at any point. Typically, such a test takes about 2 to 5 days.

The effective stress equation for the CD test is given by:

$\overline{\sigma}_1= \overline{\sigma}_3\tan^2 \left(45^\circ + \frac{\phi}{2}\right) + 2c'\tan \left(45^\circ + \frac{\phi}{2}\right)$

Since the effective cohesion $c' = 0$ and the effective angle of shearing resistance $\phi' = 30^\circ$, we can simplify the equation:

$\overline{\sigma}_1 = \overline{\sigma}_3 \tan^2(60^\circ) + 0$

Given that the test experienced 70 kPa of pore water pressure before failure, the effective confining pressure $\overline{\sigma}_3$ is calculated as:

$\overline{\sigma}_3 = \sigma_3 - 70$

The total axial stress at failure $\sigma_1$ is given by:

$\overline{\sigma}_1 = \sigma_1 - 70 = \sigma_3 + \sigma_d - 70$

Where:

  • $\sigma_1$ is the major principal stress
  • $\sigma_3$ is the minor principal stress
  • $\sigma_d$ is the deviatoric stress at failure (360 kPa is given)

Now, setting up the equation for failure with the known deviatoric stress and substituting the given values:

$(\sigma_3 + 360 - 70) = (\sigma_3 - 70) \tan^2(60^\circ)$

Solving for $\sigma_3$:

⇒ $\sigma_3 + 290 = (\sigma_3 - 70) \times 3$

⇒ $\sigma_3 + 290 = 3\sigma_3 - 210$

⇒ $290 + 210 = 3\sigma_3 - \sigma_3$

⇒ $500 = 2\sigma_3$

⇒ $\sigma_3 = 250 \, \text{kPa}$

Using the adjusted effective confining stress $\sigma_3 = 250 \, \text{kPa}$, we find the major principal stress at failure $\sigma_1$ without error:

$\sigma_1 = \sigma_3 \tan^2(60^\circ)$

Since $\sigma_1 = \sigma_3 + \sigma_d$:

⇒ $250 + \sigma_d = 250 \times 3$

Therefore:

⇒ $\sigma_d = 750 - 250$

⇒ $\sigma_d = 500 \, \text{kPa}$

content: A saturated compressible clay layer of thickness h is sandwiched between two sand layers, as shown in the figure. Initially, the total vertical stress and pore water pressure at point P, which is located at the mid-depth of the clay layer were 150 kPa and 25 kPa, respectively. Construction of a building caused an additional total vertical stress of 100 kPa at P. When the vertical effective stress at P is 175 kPa. The percentage of consolidation in the clay layr at P is _________. (in integer)
explanation:
We know degree of consolidation,

$$\% U = {{{u_1} - u} \over {{u_i}}} \times 100$$

ui = initial excess pore water pressure i.e. 100 kPa

Initial total stress, $$\sigma$$ = 150 kPa

Initial effective stress, $$\sigma$$'0 = $$\sigma$$ $$-$$ u = 150 $$-$$ 25 = 125 kPa

Total stress after construction of building = 150 + 100 = 250 kPa

When the vertical effective stress at P is 175 kPa, the change in effective stress is 175 $$-$$ 125 = 50 kPa, so during this, the change in pore water pressure is

$$\therefore$$ $$\Delta$$$$\sigma$$ = $$\Delta$$$$\sigma$$' + $$\Delta$$u

(250 $$-$$ 150) = (175 $$-$$ 125) + $$\Delta$$u

$$\therefore$$ $$\Delta$$u = 50 kPa

$$\therefore$$ %u = $${{100 - 50} \over {100}} \times 100 = 50\% $$
